THE COURT CONSIDERED ALL REQUIRED FACTORS OF THE LAW. THE COURT FINDS THAT PRISON IS CONSISTENT WITH THE PURPOSE OF R. C. 2929.11. THE COURT IMPOSES A PRISON SENTENCE AT THE LORAIN CORRECTIONAL INSTITUTION OF 30 MONTH(S). COUNT 1: F4, 18 MONTH(S); UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. COUNT 3: F5, 12 MONTH(S); UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. COUNTS 1 AND 3 ARE TO BE SERVED CONSECUTIVE TO EACH OTHER. THE COURT IMPOSES PRISON TERMS CONSECUTIVELY FINDING THAT CONSECUTIVE SERVICE OF THE PRISON TERM IS NECESSARY TO PROTECT THE PUBLIC FROM FUTURE CRIME OR TO PUNISH DEFENDANT; THAT THE CONSECUTIVE SENTENCES ARE NOT DISPROPORTIONATE TO THE SERIOUSNESS OF DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT AND TO THE DANGER DEFENDANT POSES TO THE PUBLIC; AND THAT, THE DEFENDANT COMMITTED ONE OR MORE OF THE MULTIPLE OFFENSES WHILE THE DEFENDANT WAS AWAITING TRIAL OR SENTENCING OR WAS UNDER A COMMUNITY CONTROL OR WAS UNDER POST-RELEASE CONTROL FOR A PRIOR OFFENSE, OR DEFENDANT'S HISTORY OF CRIMINAL CONDUCT DEMONSTRATES THAT CONSECUTIVE SENTENCES ARE NECESSARY TO PROTECT THE PUBLIC FROM FUTURE CRIME BY DEFENDANT. AS A RESULT OF THE CONVICTION(S) IN THIS CASE AND THE IMPOSITION OF A PRISON SENTENCE, AND PURSUANT TO R.C. 2967.28(F)(4)(C), THE DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBJECT TO A PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL OF: UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. nightmare_events: 02/27/2025 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. A notice of appeal is considered filed when the notice of appeal is received by the clerk's office, not when the notice of appeal is mailed. App.R. 13(A). THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. 05/29/2025 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. CONDITIONS AND TERMS OF PROBATION ARE SUBJECT TO MODIFICATION BY THE PROBATION OFFICER AND APPROVAL OF THE COURT. VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS MAY RESULT IN EXTENSION OF THE PERIOD OF SUPERVISION, MORE RESTRICTIVE SANCTIONS, OR A PRISON TERM OF 12 MONTHS AS APPROVED BY LAW. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. FAILURE OF THE COMPLAINING WITNESS TO APPEAR, WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, WILL RESULT IN DISMISSAL OF THE CASE FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE. 10/28/2025 CPDL2 10/28/2025 09:15:08
